body.title: CLOSED: Implement IMAP IDLE on bus client/listener side
body.result: idle_wait + run_forever — sub-ms wakeup in stub, 2s-poll fallback in prod, 6 tests, ca0077b
body.description:
Implement IMAP IDLE on the client side of the bus router/listener. Currently the router polls on a timer; IDLE gives true server-push behavior.

IDLE pattern: client sends IDLE, connection stays open, server pushes unsolicited EXISTS when a message arrives, client wakes, sends DONE, fetches, processes. Zero CPU cost at rest. Sub-millisecond wakeup.

RFC 2177: server may drop IDLE after 29 minutes — client must periodically send DONE + re-enter IDLE to keep alive. Handle this automatically.

Library: aioimaplib (async) or imapclient (sync, has idle() context manager).

Both Igor and the Librarian inherit this — any rack participant sitting on a mailbox gets event-driven wakeup instead of a poll loop.

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/bus/router.py, agent_datacenter/announce/listener.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; bus/ owns comms:// routing
**Scope boundary:** client-side IDLE only; stub already implements server-side
**Test plan:** listener wakes within 100ms of message arrival; 29-minute keepalive re-enters IDLE correctly; disconnect handled cleanly
